agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core e-commerce/logistics capability that allows customers to reverse a purchase before fulfillment is complete. It intersects ordering, payment, inventory, and fulfillment bounded contexts. The PM's requirement is stated without constraints, leaving significant ambiguity around cancellation windows, refund policies, and order lifecycle stage eligibility.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A confirmed request by a user to purchase one or more items, tracked through a lifecycle from placement to completion or cancellation."},{"term":"Order Cancellation","definition":"A user-initiated action that terminates an active order, halting further fulfillment processing and triggering applicable refund logic."},{"term":"Order Status","definition":"The current lifecycle stage of an order (e.g., Pending, Confirmed, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled)."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period or set of order statuses during which cancellation is permitted."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the user following a successful cancellation, subject to refund policy rules."},{"term":"Fulfillment","definition":"The process of picking, packing, and shipping an order to the customer."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order can only be cancelled while it is in a cancellable status (before physical fulfillment has begun).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Once goods have left the warehouse or a service has been rendered, the cancellation concept no longer applies — it becomes a return or dispute. Allowing cancellation of shipped orders would corrupt fulfillment state.","testable_assertion":"Calling cancel on an order with status in {Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled} must be rejected with an error."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A cancelled order must not transition to any status other than Cancelled. Cancellation is a terminal state.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing a cancelled order to be reopened or shipped would violate data integrity and could result in unauthorized charges or shipments.","testable_assertion":"Any state transition attempt from Cancelled to another status must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Cancellation must trigger a refund process for any payment already captured.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund policy (full vs. partial, timing, method) is a business policy decision that may vary by product type, region, or promotion. The linkage is required but the terms are policy-driven.","testable_assertion":"When an order with captured payment is cancelled, a refund record is created referencing the original payment."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Only the user who placed the order (or an authorized agent) may cancel it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing unauthorized cancellation would be a security and trust violation — one user could cancel another's order.","testable_assertion":"A cancel request from a user whose ID does not match the order's owner (and who lacks admin/agent role) must be rejected with 403."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Cancellation of an order must release any reserved inventory back to available stock.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Failure to release inventory on cancellation leads to phantom stock depletion, preventing other customers from purchasing available items.","testable_assertion":"After cancellation, the inventory count for each item in the order increases by the cancelled quantity."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Each cancellation must be recorded with a timestamp, acting user, and reason (if provided).","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Audit trail depth is a business/compliance policy; the need for a reason field and its required vs. optional status is policy-driven.","testable_assertion":"The cancellation record contains a non-null timestamp, a non-null actor ID, and a reason field (which may be null if reason is optional)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A user has an order in 'Pending' or 'Confirmed' status","when":"The user requests cancellation of that order","then":"The order status transitions to 'Cancelled', a refund is initiated for any captured payment, reserved inventory is released, and a cancellation record is persisted."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A user has an order in 'Shipped' or 'Delivered' status","when":"The user requests cancellation of that order","then":"The system rejects the request with a clear error message indicating the order is no longer eligible for cancellation and suggests the returns process instead."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A user has an order that is already 'Cancelled'","when":"The user requests cancellation of that order","then":"The system returns an idempotent success or a clear message that the order is already cancelled. No duplicate refund is created."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"User A has an active order","when":"User B (not an admin or authorized agent) requests cancellation of User A's order","then":"The system rejects the request with a 403 Forbidden error. The order remains unchanged."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A user has an order in 'Processing' status (being picked/packed but not yet shipped)","when":"The user requests cancellation","then":"The system either cancels the order (if fulfillment can be halted) or rejects with a message explaining the order is too far along — behavior depends on ambiguous cancellation window policy (see escalations)."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A user cancels an order containing 3 units of Item X","when":"The cancellation completes successfully","then":"Available inventory for Item X increases by 3."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A user cancels an order that was paid via credit card","when":"The cancellation completes successfully","then":"A refund record is created for the captured amount, and the payment gateway refund is initiated."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"The payment gateway is temporarily unavailable during cancellation","when":"The user requests cancellation","then":"The order is still marked Cancelled, and the refund is queued for retry. The user is informed that the refund is being processed."},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An order contains multiple items, some from different fulfillment centers","when":"The user requests cancellation","then":"The entire order is cancelled atomically — partial cancellation is not implied by this requirement (see escalations for partial cancellation ambiguity)."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"User cancels an order during a payment authorization hold (not yet captured)","expected_behavior":"The authorization hold is voided rather than refunded. No refund record is created; instead an authorization reversal is recorded."},{"scenario":"User cancels an order that had a promotional discount or coupon applied","expected_behavior":"The coupon or promotion usage is reversed so the user can reuse it. If the promotion has since expired, business policy determines whether the coupon is still restored."},{"scenario":"User cancels an order and immediately re-places the same order","expected_behavior":"The re-placed order is treated as a new order. Inventory released by cancellation is available for the new order but not reserved exclusively."},{"scenario":"Concurrent cancellation requests for the same order (double-click, network retry)","expected_behavior":"The system handles this idempotently — the first request cancels, the second returns success without side effects (no duplicate refund, no duplicate inventory release)."},{"scenario":"Order is in 'Processing' and warehouse has already picked items","expected_behavior":"System must check with fulfillment subsystem. If items are picked but not shipped, cancellation may or may not be possible depending on warehouse integration capabilities."},{"scenario":"Cancellation requested during a system-wide inventory sync","expected_behavior":"Inventory release from cancellation must be consistent — either fully applied or fully rolled back. No partial inventory updates."},{"scenario":"User cancels an order that was placed with store credit or gift card","expected_behavior":"The store credit or gift card balance is restored rather than issuing a cash/card refund."},{"scenario":"Admin/support agent cancels on behalf of a user","expected_behavior":"The cancellation is permitted and the audit record reflects the agent as the actor, not the customer."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellations directly reduce revenue but timely refund processing reduces chargeback risk and associated fees. Proper inventory release prevents lost sales from phantom stock depletion.","operational":"Cancellation logic must integrate with payment, inventory, and fulfillment systems. Poor integration leads to manual reconciliation work. Idempotency failures could cause double refunds.","user":"Cancellation capability is a baseline user expectation. Lack of self-service cancellation drives support ticket volume. Clear communication about cancellation eligibility reduces frustration.","risk":"Primary risks: double refunds from race conditions, inventory leaks from failed releases, unauthorized cancellations. Secondary: unclear cancellation windows cause user confusion and support escalations.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Gain self-service order cancellation, reducing wait times and support dependency. Must receive clear feedback on eligibility and refund timelines."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Reduced ticket volume for cancellation requests. Need visibility into cancellation audit trail for dispute resolution."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Must reconcile refunds against cancellations. Need reliable audit records for reporting and compliance."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Must receive timely cancellation signals to halt picking/packing. Late cancellations after shipment create return logistics costs."},{"group":"Product/Engineering","impact":"Must implement cancellation across multiple bounded contexts (orders, payments, inventory, fulfillment) with transactional consistency."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The PM's requirement is a single sentence with no specification of cancellation window, refund policy, partial cancellation support, or order lifecycle definition. Multiple critical business decisions are left undefined. While the core concept is clear, the implementation-critical details require stakeholder input before acceptance criteria can be finalized.","high_confidence_areas":["Cancellation is a terminal state","Only authorized users can cancel","Inventory must be released on cancellation","Audit trail is required","Cancellation of already-shipped orders is not cancellation (it's a return)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact set of cancellable statuses — especially 'Processing' stage","Partial cancellation (individual line items) vs. whole-order only","Refund method matching (original payment method vs. store credit)","Whether cancellation reason is required or optional","Time-based cancellation window (e.g., 30 minutes after placement) vs. status-based only","Coupon/promotion restoration policy on cancellation"],"assumptions":["The system has a defined order lifecycle with discrete statuses","Payment capture may or may not have occurred at cancellation time","The requirement refers to whole-order cancellation, not partial/line-item cancellation","'Cancel' means user-initiated; system-initiated cancellations (fraud, stock-out) are a separate feature"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The cancellation window is undefined. It is unclear which order statuses are cancellable — particularly whether 'Processing' (picked but not shipped) orders can be cancelled. Status-based vs. time-based windows are not specified.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Warehouse/Fulfillment","Customer Support"],"options":["Status-based only: allow cancellation for Pending and Confirmed, reject for Processing onwards","Status-based with Processing: allow cancellation up to and including Processing if fulfillment can be halted","Time-based: allow cancellation within N minutes of placement regardless of status","Hybrid: status-based with a time-based grace period for Processing orders"],"recommendation":"Start with status-based cancellation (Pending + Confirmed), and treat Processing as non-cancellable in v1. Add Processing-stage cancellation in v2 once warehouse integration supports halt signals."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Partial cancellation (cancelling individual items from a multi-item order) is not addressed. The requirement says 'cancel their order' (singular, whole order) but users commonly expect line-item cancellation.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance/Accounting","Warehouse/Fulfillment"],"options":["Whole-order cancellation only in v1","Support partial (line-item) cancellation from day one","Whole-order only, with a follow-up ticket for partial cancellation"],"recommendation":"Ship whole-order cancellation first. Partial cancellation introduces significant complexity in refund calculation, inventory management, and fulfillment signaling. Scope it as a separate feature."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Refund policy details are unspecified — full refund vs. partial (e.g., restocking fee), refund to original payment method vs. store credit, and refund timing guarantees are all undefined.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance/Accounting"],"options":["Always full refund to original payment method","Full refund but allow store credit option","Configurable refund policy per cancellation reason or product category"],"recommendation":"Default to full refund to original payment method for user-initiated cancellations. This is the least surprising behavior and can be refined with policy rules later."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core e-commerce/logistics capability that allows customers to reverse a purchase before fulfillment is complete. It intersects ordering, payment, inventory, and fulfillment bounded contexts. The PM's requirement is stated without constraints, leaving significant ambiguity around cancellation windows, refund policies, and order lifecycle stage eligibility.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","

content.thinking:
The PM says "Users should be able to cancel their order." This is a vague requirement. Let me analyze this from a domain perspective.

This is ambiguous - many details are missing. Let me classify: the requirement is vague and can be interpreted multiple ways. This is `ambiguity`.

Let me produce my analysis.
